evidence_excerpt: Ocular infection and inflammation resolved after vitrectomy and antifungal therapy, but vision remained poor (left eye 20/500) due to foveal thinning and photoreceptor disruption; hypercortisolism resolved after GKS and medical therapy.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Persistent visual impairment in the left eye (20/500) despite resolution of retinal infiltrates and vitritis, indicating ongoing residual symptoms.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: Her eye symptoms improved following the vitrectomy and systemic antifungal therapy; discharged after 6 days of IV fluconazole.
Two months after discharge, vision had improved slightly to 20/500 with complete resolution of retinal infiltrate.
Visual outcome was poor due to foveal thinning and photoreceptor disruption at 2.5 months.
Gamma knife surgery was performed two days after vitrectomy (day 9) for recurrent pituitary tumor causing Cushing’s disease.
Hypercortisolism resolved 3 months after GKS with cortisol-lowering therapy; by day 147 ACTH and cortisol levels were in normal range.

notes_on_uncertainty: Follow-up is limited to approximately 5 months after diagnosis; no data beyond 12 months. Functional status and symptom burden are inferred from unilateral poor visual acuity and clinician assessments, with no explicit activity-of-daily-living descriptions.
